[PDF_PAGE 208 | score=15] This spell allows the caster to selectively warp the fabric of space, folding it into higher dimensions. This effect can be best explained through an example. If an ant crawling along the west edge of a map decided to travel to the east edge of the map, it would have to crawl the full width of the map. But if the map was folded in two so that the east and west edges were touching, the ant would travel almost no distance at all. The ant’s world (the map) would have been folded through the third di- mension. The dimensional folding spell does something similar with the three-dimensional world: it folds it through a higher dimension (the fourth), allowing instantaneous travel between two locale
